title: Petroleum Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can run reservoir-simulation sweeps and drilling-data analysis, but well-site safety decisions and field operations judgment remain firmly human.
rationales.toolMaturity: Reservoir-simulation and drilling-optimization software has AI-assisted modules, but adoption across the industry is uneven and specialist.
rationales.adoption: Adoption concentrates at major operators with large digital-oilfield programs; independents and smaller operators lag.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run reservoir-model sweeps and flag production anomalies, but rig-site safety calls and well-control decisions need an engineer on location or on call.

tasksGrowing: Field operations judgment under changing conditions
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 17-2171, p25-p75 rounded, upper end reflects operator market postings
insight: Reservoir simulation and drilling-data analysis increasingly run on AI-assisted models, but a well-control decision at the rig site still needs a petroleum engineer who owns the physical risk.
